title: Poikilacanthus glandulosus
intro: Poikilacanthus glandulosus, also known as Eremophila or Emu Bush, is a plant species native to the southwestern regions of Australia. The health benefits of Poikilacanthus glandulosus include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Poikilacanthus glandulosus include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
taxonomy: Poikilacanthus glandulosus is classified within the family Acanthaceae. This plant belongs to the order Lamiales. It is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida and the division Magnoliophyta. Poikilacanthus glandulosus is a species of flowering plant. It is native to tropical regions.
